sources.name: Hyperliquid metaAndAssetCtxs (perp universe + per-coin market ctx)
sources.rate_limit: weight 20 of 1200/min/IP (docs); no rate-limit headers exposed (CloudFront)
sources.value_for_decisions: ONE keyless call = the whole HL perp market: confirmed exact field names funding (hourly rate), openInterest (in COIN units — multiply by markPx for notional), premium, oraclePx, markPx, midPx, impactPxs (bid/ask impact prices = built-in slippage estimate), dayNtlVlm, dayBaseVlm, prevDayPx; universe entries carry name, szDecimals, maxLeverage, marginTableId, and isDelisted:true on dead coins (MATIC confirmed live). 230 perps mainnet; ctxs array index-aligned with universe array. NEW-LISTING DETECTION: no listing timestamps anywhere — poll and diff universe names (new coins append; cache previous set); approximate listing time via earliest 1d candleSnapshot candle.
sources.notes: Response is [meta, assetCtxs] tuple. Testnet (api.hyperliquid-testnet.xyz/info) verified: identical shape but only 208 perps and sandbox prices/funding/OI that diverge wildly from reality — use MAINNET for all decision data, testnet only for tradability checks and execution.

sources.name: Hyperliquid l2Book (orderbook depth)
sources.rate_limit: weight 2 (cheapest tier) of 1200/min/IP — can poll every coin every few seconds
sources.value_for_decisions: HL-native depth for bid/ask imbalance, spread, and slippage-at-size on the venue you actually execute on. levels = [bids[], asks[]], each level {px, sz, n} (n = order count). Up to 20 levels per side. This is the input Binance/Bybit providers fundamentally cannot supply: Binance depth says nothing about what your HL market order will pay.
sources.notes: Weight 2 makes this + allMids + clearinghouseState the endpoints to poll aggressively; everything else costs 20.

sources.name: Hyperliquid recentTrades (tape)
sources.rate_limit: weight 20 + 1 extra weight per 20 items returned (docs); prefer WS trades channel for streaming
sources.value_for_decisions: Last ~500 trades with side (B=buy aggressor/A=sell), px, sz, time — taker-flow / aggression imbalance on HL itself. Bonus: 'users' exposes [buyer, seller] addresses, so you can harvest real active wallets to feed clearinghouseState/userFills (whale watching) with zero auth.
sources.notes: Endpoint name is 'recentTrades' (not 'trades' — 'trades' is the WS channel name).

sources.name: Hyperliquid candleSnapshot (OHLCV)
sources.rate_limit: weight 20 + 1 extra weight per 60 candles returned (docs)
sources.value_for_decisions: HL-native OHLCV: t/T open-close ms, o/c/h/l strings, v = base volume, n = trade count. Intervals (docs+SDK): 1m,3m,5m,15m,30m,1h,2h,4h,8h,12h,1d,3d,1w,1M (1m and 1h verified live). HISTORY DEPTH VERIFIED: only the most recent ~5000 candles per interval — a wide 1m request returned 5046 candles starting just 3.5 days back. So 1h ≈ 208 days, 1d ≈ 13y (i.e., full listing history). Earliest 1d candle ≈ listing date — the only way to get perp/spot listing timestamps.
sources.notes: For deep momentum/vol lookbacks on majors, the existing Binance klines provider still wins on 1m history depth; HL candles suffice for >=1h work.

sources.name: Hyperliquid fundingHistory (realized hourly funding)
sources.value_for_decisions: One record per HOUR (HL funding interval = 1h, unlike Binance 8h/4h). fundingRate + premium per period — funding trend/mean-reversion features and realistic carry-cost backtesting for HL positions. 0.0000125/h is the baseline (= 0.01%/8h equivalent).
sources.notes: This is HL-realized funding; the repo currently computes funding from Binance/Bybit which is the WRONG number for cost on an HL position.

sources.name: Hyperliquid predictedFundings (CROSS-VENUE predicted funding)
sources.value_for_decisions: GOLD for this repo: one keyless call returns predicted next funding for EVERY coin on Binance (BinPerp), Bybit (BybitPerp) and Hyperliquid (HlPerp) with nextFundingTime and fundingIntervalHours. Live sample shows real divergence (0G: Binance +0.0000109/4h vs HL -0.0000916/1h) — direct funding-arb / carry-divergence signal, and it can replace per-symbol funding calls to the Binance/Bybit providers for screening (1 call vs N).
sources.notes: Shape is nested tuples: [[coin, [[venue, {…}], …]], …]. Normalize rates by fundingIntervalHours before comparing venues.

sources.name: Hyperliquid spotMeta / spotMetaAndAssetCtxs (spot universe + ctx)
sources.value_for_decisions: 303 spot pairs live. tokens[] (name, index, tokenId, evmContract, deployerTradingFeeShare) + universe[] pairs ({tokens:[base,quote], name:'PURR/USDC' or '@N', index}) + ctxs with markPx/midPx/dayNtlVlm/circulatingSupply/totalSupply (spot has supply data perps don't). NEW-LISTING DETECTION: indexes are append-only — persist max pair index and max token index, anything above is a new listing; no timestamps in meta (use first candle of the '@N' pair to date it). Both endpoints verified 200.
sources.notes: Most pairs are named '@N' (use tokens[] indexes to resolve the human name); allMids uses the same '@N' keys for spot.

sources.name: Hyperliquid clearinghouseState + userFills + userFunding (account/PnL by ADDRESS, no key)
sources.rate_limit: clearinghouseState weight 2; userFills/userFunding weight 20 + 1 per 20 items (docs)
sources.value_for_decisions: CONFIRMED: any public 0x address works with zero auth (tested an address harvested from recentTrades). clearinghouseState gives accountValue, withdrawable, marginUsed, assetPositions (entryPx, szi, leverage, liquidationPx, unrealizedPnl, marginUsed when positions open). userFills gives dir ('Open Long'/'Close Long'), closedPnl, fee — full PnL journal. userFunding gives per-settlement funding paid/received (delta.usdc, szi, fundingRate). Powers: (a) the repo's own paper-PnL tracking on testnet with just the wallet address, (b) keyless whale/copy-trade monitoring on mainnet.
sources.notes: MCPenny's journal/PnL task can be built entirely on these three — no private key needed for reads, key only for the existing execution write path.

sources.name: Hyperliquid allMids + perpsAtOpenInterestCap
sources.sample_response_snippet: allMids: {"BTC":"61628.5","@1":"...","#1000":"0.5",...} (726 keys: perp names, '@N' spot, '#N' HIP-3 builder-dex) | perpsAtOpenInterestCap: ["CANTO","FTM","JELLY","LOOM","RLB","ZEREBRO"]
sources.value_for_decisions: allMids = every mid on the venue in one weight-2 call (726 assets) — cheapest heartbeat/price poller. perpsAtOpenInterestCap is an HL-only tradability gate: coins at the OI cap reject new position-increasing orders, so crypto_screen should hard-filter these six (live list) before recommending entries.
sources.notes: allMids spot keys are '@index' — join against spotMeta.universe to resolve names.

sources.name: Hyperliquid WebSocket (mainnet wss://api.hyperliquid.xyz/ws, testnet wss://api.hyperliquid-testnet.xyz/ws)
sources.rate_limit: Docs (fetched live): max 10 WS connections/IP, 30 new connections/min, 1000 subscriptions, 10 unique users across user-specific subs, 2000 client->server messages/min, 100 inflight posts. Server pushes on change; send {"method":"ping"} keepalive (server closes after ~60s idle)
sources.value_for_decisions: ALL SIX channels verified live in one connection (websocket-client 1.9.0 already in the repo venv): allMids (all mids dict on every tick), l2Book (full book snapshots), trades (tape incl. users), candle (in-progress bar updates), activeAssetCtx (streaming funding/OI/premium — the metaAndAssetCtxs ctx for one coin), bbo (top-of-book only, lightest). Each sub acks with channel 'subscriptionResponse'. This is the always-on poller: ~10 coins x (activeAssetCtx + bbo + trades) stays far under the 1000-sub limit and costs zero REST weight.
sources.notes: User channels also exist (userFills, userFundings, orderUpdates, userEvents, notification, webData2) keyed by address only — not exercised in this test but same no-auth model. 'post' method lets you tunnel info requests over WS.

sources.name: Hyperliquid testnet info API (paper-trading parity)
sources.rate_limit: same documented 1200 weight/min/IP scheme as mainnet
sources.value_for_decisions: Identical request/response shapes, but the DATA is sandbox: 208 perps vs 230 on mainnet, and prices/funding/OI/volume diverge wildly from real markets (testnet thin books, default 0.0000125 funding). Rule for MCPenny: DECIDE on mainnet data, EXECUTE + check tradability (paper_pairs, lot sizes, clearinghouseState of the paper wallet) on testnet. Never feed testnet ctx values into the scoring engine.
sources.notes: Verified live; testnet universe is a subset+lag of mainnet, so a mainnet-screened coin may not exist on testnet — keep the existing HL-listed filter pointed at testnet meta for executability.

sources.name: Hyperliquid leaderboard (unofficial stats endpoint)
sources.rate_limit: undocumented (not part of the 1200-weight API); large payload (~MBs) — cache hours, poll at most a few times/day
sources.value_for_decisions: Full trader leaderboard: ethAddress, accountValue, and day/week/month/allTime {pnl, roi, vlm}. Pipe top-roi addresses into the keyless clearinghouseState/userFills endpoints for live smart-money positioning (what are profitable HL whales long/short right now) — a sentiment feature no Binance endpoint offers.
sources.notes: UNOFFICIAL (powers app.hyperliquid.xyz UI), undocumented, could change without notice — treat as best-effort enrichment, never a hard dependency.

sources.name: Hyperliquid liquidation data (global feed) — NOT AVAILABLE keyless
sources.request_body: {"type":"userFills","user":"0x..."} — liquidation fills carry a 'liquidation' object ({liquidatedUser, markPx, method}) and dir like 'Liquidated ...'
sources.value_for_decisions: There is NO public 'liquidatable users' or global liquidation-stream endpoint in the info API — confirmed absent from docs; liquidations only surface per-address in userFills/userNonFundingLedgerUpdates and as ordinary prints on the trades tape (unflagged). Workarounds: (a) compute liquidation proximity yourself from clearinghouseState.assetPositions[].liquidationPx of tracked whale addresses (all keyless, verified above); (b) the markPx/oraclePx gap + OI drops in activeAssetCtx as a liquidation-cascade proxy; (c) third-party aggregators (Coinalyze free key) for cross-venue liquidation totals.
sources.notes: verified=false because I could not find a liquidated fill to fetch during this session; the field shape is from official docs. Everything recommended above it is live-verified.

sources.name: Hyperliquid rate limits (official docs, fetched live this session)
sources.rate_limit: REST: aggregated 1200 weight/min/IP. Weight 2: l2Book, allMids, clearinghouseState, orderStatus, spotClearinghouseState, exchangeStatus. Weight 60: userRole. All other info types: weight 20. Extra: +1 weight per 20 items returned for recentTrades/userFills/fundingHistory/userFunding/historicalOrders/etc.; +1 per 60 candles for candleSnapshot. Explorer requests weight 40. WS: 10 conns/IP, 30 new/min, 1000 subs, 2000 msgs/min, 100 inflight posts. Address-based limits apply only to EXCHANGE (trading) actions, not info reads.
sources.sample_response_snippet: "REST requests share an aggregated weight limit of 1200 per minute. ... The following info requests have weight 2: l2Book, allMids, clearinghouseState, orderStatus, spotClearinghouseState, exchangeStatus. The following info requests have weight 60: userRole. All other documented info requests have weight 20."
sources.value_for_decisions: Budget math for an always-on poller: metaAndAssetCtxs every 15s (4x20=80/min) + l2Book for 20 coins every 5s (20x2x12=480/min) + allMids every 2s (60/min) ≈ 620/1200 — comfortably free. No rate-limit response headers exist (checked live: only CloudFront headers), so do client-side budgeting; on 429 back off 10s. WS data pushes cost zero REST weight — prefer WS for hot coins.
sources.notes: Fetched and quoted from the live docs page during this task.

summary: Hyperliquid's public POST /info API is a fully keyless, production-grade market-data source — I live-verified 13 REST request types on mainnet, the testnet mirror, and 6 WebSocket channels from this machine with zero auth, all returning 200 with documented shapes. Key confirmations: (1) metaAndAssetCtxs returns exact fields funding/openInterest/premium/oraclePx/markPx/midPx/impactPxs/dayNtlVlm/dayBaseVlm/prevDayPx per coin, index-aligned with a universe[] carrying maxLeverage and isDelisted (MATIC shows isDelisted:true live) — 230 perps mainnet vs 208 testnet, and testnet ctx values are sandbox garbage, so decide on mainnet, execute on testnet. (2) l2Book (weight 2) gives 20-level depth with per-level order counts for imbalance/slippage. (3) recentTrades exposes counterparty addresses — a free whale-address harvester. (4) candleSnapshot supports 1m..1M but only the most recent ~5000 candles per interval (verified: 1m history starts 3.5 days back), so keep Binance for deep 1m history. (5) fundingHistory is hourly; predictedFundings is the standout: one call returns predicted funding for every coin on Binance+Bybit+Hyperliquid with intervals — a built-in cross-venue carry/divergence signal that can replace per-symbol funding calls. (6) spotMeta(AndAssetCtxs) covers 303 pairs with supply data; new listings are detected by diffing append-only universe/token indexes (no listing timestamps exist; date a listing via its earliest candle). (7) clearinghouseState/userFills/userFunding work for ANY address with no key — confirmed live with an address harvested from the tape; this powers both the repo's paper-PnL journal and mainnet smart-money tracking, especially combined with the verified unofficial stats-data leaderboard. (8) No global liquidation feed exists; proxy via tracked whales' liquidationPx or third parties. (9) Limits (official docs, fetched live): 1200 weight/min/IP, weight 2 for l2Book/allMids/clearinghouseState, 20 for the rest, +size-based surcharges; WS allows 10 conns/1000 subs and is the right transport for an always-on poller (allMids, l2Book, trades, candle, activeAssetCtx, bbo all verified streaming). Versus the repo's existing Binance/Bybit providers (ohlcv/quote/funding/OI/long-short ratio): those describe OTHER venues — HL funding is hourly and materially different (live: 0G at -0.0000916/1h on HL vs +0.0000109/4h on Binance), HL-native OI/depth/impactPxs are what determine squeeze risk and slippage for orders actually placed on Hyperliquid, and perpsAtOpenInterestCap (live: 6 coins blocked) plus maxLeverage/isDelisted are HL-only tradability gates the screener should enforce. Keep Binance/Bybit only for long/short-ratio sentiment, deep candle history, and as the cross-venue legs that predictedFundings already half-replaces.
